weekly.intro: 99 AI items this week (65 India-relevant) across 9 sources.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.summary: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company b…

items.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
items.summary: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

items.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
items.summary: We’re announcing new capabilities in Managed Agents in Gemini API so developers can build reliable, production-ready agents.

items.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
items.summary: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

items.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
items.summary: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

items.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
items.summary: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

items.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
items.summary: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

items.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

items.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
items.summary: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the…

items.summary: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

items.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
items.summary: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.
